citation_short: U.S. State Dept., 'The Arab-Israeli War of 1948'
title: Milestones in the History of U.S. Foreign Relations - The Arab-Israeli War of 1948
author_or_org: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State
strengths: Useful for a concise official synopsis of the civil-war to interstate-war transition and regional participants.
limitations: Not suitable for subnational denominators or episode numerators.
notes: Used only for conflict-family overview and phase structure.

title: 1948 Arab-Israeli War | Summary, Outcome, Casualties, & Timeline
geographic_scope: Regional war / Mandatory Palestine and neighboring states
strengths: Good for formal war dates, armistice end date, and a high-level timeline including Operation Dani.
limitations: Not an episode-level casualty or exposure source.
notes: Used for conflict-family framing and date anchors.

title: Operation Dani
author_or_org: Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question (Palquest)
geographic_scope: Central Palestine, especially Lydda-Ramla and the Jerusalem corridor
strengths: Clear operational split between stage 1 (Lydda-Ramla) and stage 2 (Ramallah-al-Latrun highway), with unit composition and affected villages.
limitations: Does not provide a full numeric death series or force-presence time series.
notes: Key source for why e04 and e05 should be separate episodes.

citation_short: Morris 2004, The Birth of the Palestinian Refugee Problem Revisited
title: The Birth of the Palestinian Refugee Problem Revisited
strengths: Best single scholarly source for town population, timing of outflow, expulsion evidence, and why status coding is difficult.
limitations: Not a named-victim registry; direct deaths are discussed narratively rather than separated into audit-ready civilian/combatant counts.
notes: Primary denominator source for e04 and the main source for explaining why core DR must remain null.

title: The Fall of Lydda
strengths: Local perspective with concrete figures for Israeli attackers, regular Arab troops, and a civilian-death claim.
limitations: Eyewitness-based retrospective with strong narrative framing; status categories are not independently audited.
notes: Used as a lower-bound source cluster for direct deaths and for military-denominator bounding.

title: Lydda, 9-13 July 1948
author_or_org: Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question (Palquest)
geographic_scope: Lydda, with reference to Ramla and the expulsion route
strengths: Useful secondary synthesis for Lydda baseline population, high-end direct-death claims, and expulsion scale.
limitations: Not a primary casualty dataset; mixes narrative summary with interpretive language, and some totals blend city and march contexts if used incautiously.
notes: Used mainly as an upper-bound/context source rather than the sole numerator source.

title: Palestinians in Israel's 'Mixed Cities'
author_or_org: Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question (Palquest)
geographic_scope: Lydda and Ramla among other cities
strengths: Convenient baseline figure for Ramla and postwar remainder context.
limitations: Secondary summary, not an episode-specific wartime count.
notes: Used only as a baseline cross-check for town population scale.

citation_short: National Library of Israel, 'Supply convoy to Jerusalem, 1948'
title: Supply convoy to Jerusalem, 1948
strengths: Useful for corridor chronology and why the Jerusalem road needs its own moving footprint.
limitations: No episode-level population or casualty counts.

title: al-Latrun
author_or_org: Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question (Palquest)
geographic_scope: al-Latrun salient and road to Jerusalem
strengths: Good for identifying repeated Israeli assaults on a geographically compact salient.
limitations: Not a full incident dataset or denominator source.
notes: Used to justify the distinct corridor episode.

title: Overall Chronology - Jerusalem overview entry
author_or_org: Interactive Encyclopedia of the Palestine Question (Palquest)
strengths: Provides a useful late-1940s population context with an explicit warning that British administrative boundaries were gerrymandered.
limitations: Boundary caveat means the figures are a warning sign, not a ready-made denominator.
notes: Supports why e02 needs bespoke geometry rather than municipal totals alone.
